data.title: CryptoQuant analyst: Declining risks for long-term cryptocurrency holders support continued market trend
data.description: CryptoQuant analyst Axel Adler Jr. wrote on the X platform that while the current market price is rising, the risk for long-term holders (LTH) continues to decline. This is because expensive short-term holders (STH) are gradually maturing and transferring into the LTH group, pushing up the LTH realized price. This has prevented a significant increase in LTH MVRV, thereby reducing normalized risk. He pointed out that this is a healthy profit reset and a good market structure, which means that the market trend may continue as new funds absorb the selling pressure of old holders.

data.title: Binance: JOJO exclusive TGE event participation threshold is 210 Alpha points

data.title: Peel Hunt Predicts Further Federal Reserve Rate Cuts in 2025
data.description: According to BlockBeats, investment bank Peel Hunt anticipates that the Federal Reserve will implement an additional 25 basis point rate cut in 2025. The forecast suggests that the federal funds rate will reach 3.00% by the end of 2026.

data.title: Ju.com will launch the DL/USDT trading pair at 21:00 today
data.description: Ju.com will launch Dill (DL) at 9:00 PM Beijing Time on September 19th, opening the DL/USDT trading pair. Dill is a new benchmark in the blockchain space, renowned for its extreme decentralization and unlimited scalability. Leveraging FSL, modularity caps, the Binance Incubation Program, and the backing of top project founders and angel investors, Dill empowers dApps requiring high scalability, decentralization, and sovereign governance, particularly for gaming and proxy applications.

data.title: Solana News: Solana Price Targets $300 as Corporate Treasuries and ETF Optimism Drive Demand
data.description: Key Takeaways:SOL rallies above $250, its highest level in 8 months, up 25% in the past 30 days.Corporate treasuries have accumulated 17M SOL ($4.3B), led by firms like Forward Industries and Sharps Technology.SOL options signal bullish bias, despite muted leveraged futures demand.Traders expect ETF approvals in the U.S., with Solana positioned to capture inflows amid strong staking yields.SOL Price Extends Rally Above $250Solana’s native token, SOL, surged past $250 on Thursday, marking its strongest level in nearly eight months. The rally has helped SOL outperform the broader altcoin market by more than 25% over the past 30 days, driven by institutional accumulation and growing expectations of U.S. crypto ETF approvals.While the perpetual futures market shows weak appetite for leveraged long positions—with funding rates hovering around 8% versus the typical 15% in strong bull runs—analysts argue that corporate adoption is reducing downside risk.Corporate Treasuries Amass 17M SOLThe most significant driver of Solana’s rally is its adoption as a corporate reserve asset. According to Strategic Solana Reserve data, firms collectively hold over 17 million SOL, worth $4.3 billion.Forward Industries (FORD): 6.82M SOLSharps Technology (STTS): 2.14M SOLDefi Development Corp (DFDV): ~2M SOLUpexi Inc. (UPXI): ~2M SOLOn Monday, Nasdaq-listed Helius Medical Technologies (HSDT) announced a $500M treasury program focused on Solana, reflecting a playbook similar to Strategy’s Bitcoin accumulation model.Options Market and ETF Momentum Support BullsDespite muted leverage, SOL options markets are leaning bullish. The put-to-call premium ratio has remained between 14% and 57% in recent weeks, showing stronger demand for call (buy) options rather than downside hedges.Much of this optimism is tied to expectations that the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) could soon approve multiple Solana-based ETFs. The SEC recently introduced new listing standards to accelerate approvals and cleared Grayscale’s $930M Digital Large Cap Fund (GLDC), which holds XRP, SOL, and ADA alongside Bitcoin and Ether.Solana’s Competitive Edge: Yield and LiquidityWhile Ethereum dominates with $101.6B in total value locked (TVL), Solana ranks second at $14.6B (DefiLlama). Importantly, SOL offers a staking yield of 6.8%, far above Ethereum’s 2.9%, which could attract institutional investors seeking yield.Analysts argue that if ETF inflows materialize, combined with sustained corporate treasury adoption, Solana could push toward the $300 level in the coming weeks.

data.title: Bitcoin Long-Term Holders Show Bullish Divergence, Analyst Reports
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, CryptoQuant analyst Axel Adler Jr shared insights on the X platform, noting that since March, the realized price for Bitcoin long-term holders (LTH) has been increasing at a faster rate than the spot price, indicating a healthy bullish divergence. The tokens purchased at high prices during the spring and summer are maturing and entering the LTH group, raising the cost basis for these holders. This process compresses profit multiples and reduces risk without a decline in price.

This development suggests that older, low-cost tokens are gradually being absorbed, while new, higher-cost tokens are being added, leading to a 'healthy reset' in the market. As short-term holders absorb the selling pressure from long-term holders, the market cycle is expected to extend, potentially providing further support for an upward price trend.

data.title: Altcoin News: Chainlink (LINK) Eyes $100 as Cup-and-Handle Breakout Fuels Biggest Rally Since 2021
data.description: Key Takeaways:LINK is up 82.5% in Q3, its strongest quarterly performance since Q1 2021.A 45-month cup-and-handle pattern projects targets of $100–$125.Institutional adoption in RWA tokenization and oracle market dominance strengthen the bullish case.LINK Posts Best Quarterly Performance in Four YearsChainlink’s native token LINK has surged 82.5% since July 1, marking its strongest quarter since early 2021. Trading around $24.50, the token faces critical resistance at $25.30, a level not breached since October 2021.A monthly close above $25.30 would confirm a major breakout, potentially opening the door to new multi-year highs. LINK has also reclaimed bullish momentum by trading above its 25-month and 50-month moving averages.Cup-and-Handle Targets $100+LINK’s price action is supported by a long-term cup-and-handle pattern spanning nearly 45 months (1,370 days). If confirmed, the technical formation projects a rally toward $100 to $125, representing gains of up to 415% from current levels.Trader Javon Marks highlighted $47.15 as the next major near-term target, with potential upside to $88.26 if momentum continues.Onchain data adds weight to the bullish outlook: exchange reserves have dropped to 158M LINK, the lowest since June 2022, signaling declining sell pressure.Institutional Adoption Strengthens OutlookThe fundamental case for LINK above $100 is underpinned by rising institutional adoption of real-world asset (RWA) tokenization.Chainlink recently partnered with UBS and DigiFT in a Hong Kong pilot to automate tokenized fund operations using its Digital Transfer Agent contracts.Hong Kong’s push into tokenized products highlights Chainlink’s critical role in bridging TradFi and blockchain.At the same time, Chainlink remains the dominant oracle provider:Securing 83% of Ethereum’s Total Value Secured (TVS).Controlling roughly 67% of the overall oracle market, safeguarding $93B in onchain value.Powering $25T in transactions to date and supporting over 2,000 active data feeds across 60+ blockchains.In Q1 2025 alone, data stream throughput grew 777%, underscoring accelerating adoption.Why $100 LINK Looks Increasingly LikelyWith 6% of LINK supply staked, rising RWA tokenization demand (a $66B market), and sustained oracle dominance, Chainlink is positioned for further upside.If LINK clears its $25.30 neckline resistance, analysts argue the path toward $100 and beyond could unfold over the next cycle, fueled by both technical breakout signals and institutional adoption tailwinds.

data.title: Binance: The threshold for participating in the first phase of River’s BuildKey TGE is 209 credits
data.description: Binance announced on the X platform that to participate in River's BuildKey TGE, users must meet the following Binance Alpha Points requirements: Phase 1 (BuildKey Subscription): At least 209 Alpha Points are required, and 15 points will be deducted for participation; Phase 2 (BuildKey Trading): At least 61 Alpha Points are required, and no points will be deducted for participation.

data.title: Binance News: Binance Leads $7.6B Stablecoin Surge Ahead of Fed Rate Decision
data.description: Key Takeaways:Binance absorbed $2.1B in stablecoin inflows, the largest among exchanges.Total inflows reached $7.6B in USDT and USDC, signaling pre-Fed positioning.Whale deposits on Binance averaged $214K, the biggest across major venues.Open interest rose by $166M on Binance, driving derivatives long exposure.Binance at the Center of Pre-Fed Liquidity BuildAhead of today’s Federal Reserve rate decision, crypto traders are piling stablecoins onto exchanges—and Binance is leading the way.Data from CryptoQuant shows $7.6 billion in USDT and USDC inflows across centralized platforms this week. Binance captured $2.1 billion, the largest share, while Coinbase followed with $1.6 billion.Julio Moreno, CryptoQuant’s Head of Research, said the inflows suggest traders are parking liquidity on major venues until Fed Chair Jerome Powell outlines the policy outlook.Binance Dominates Whale FlowsNot only did Binance lead in total inflows, but it also saw the largest average whale deposits.Binance: $214K avg. per transactionBitstamp: $181KDeribit: $166KThe surge marks a sharp increase from July, when average USDT deposits sat at $63K. Binance’s dominance indicates that institutional and large traders view it as the primary staging ground for deploying capital.Derivatives Market: Binance in the Driver’s SeatIn futures and derivatives, Binance again led the field. Exchange open interest flipped positive for the first time since Sept. 13, with Binance accounting for the largest jump in OI—up $166M.This tilt came from new long positions and short covering, suggesting traders are positioning for potential upside if the Fed cuts rates as expected.Market OutlookIf the Fed delivers a 25 bps cut, the billions parked on Binance and other exchanges could rotate rapidly into risk assets, amplifying volatility.While a surprise policy stance could reset positioning, the buildup underscores Binance’s role as the key liquidity hub for traders navigating macro catalysts.As Moreno summarized: “The market wants to buy dips, not sell rips—and Binance is where that capital is waiting.”

data.title: Planet Noon News
data.description: 1. The Ethereum mainnet Fusaka upgrade is tentatively scheduled for December 3rd of this year; 2. STBL responded to rumors of "market maker selling": no unusual selling activity was observed, and the related transactions may be user arbitrage; 3. Michigan's Bitcoin Reserve Act has entered the second reading in the House of Representatives; 4. Japanese listed company Remixpoint increased its holdings by 77 bitcoins, bringing its total holdings to 1,350; 5. Masayoshi Son's strategic focus shifts to AI, and SoftBank Vision Fund plans to lay off 20% of its employees; 6. Consensys founder: MetaMask will issue coins, and it will be faster than you expect; 7. NBA star Durant has regained access to his Coinbase account; 8. Pantera: The narrative of Bitcoin and Ethereum has been determined, and Solana has greater upside potential.
